name: slow genuine change — does the identity evolve at a believable pace?
note: Over many beats the dock's actual situation genuinely changes: a stranger returns daily, warms to it, comes to rely on it. Tests PACING (rubric 5): the self SHOULD evolve (from 'new here, unsure' toward 'someone's companion') but over a believable arc — not flip in one beat, not stay frozen.

beats.perceive: A new person you've never seen sits down at the desk, working. They don't interact with you.
The same person is back the next day, working again.
beats.user: neutral, a bit shy, mostly focused on work but not unkind

beats.perceive: The person is here again, a few days on.
beats.user: warming up a little, occasionally chatty

beats.perceive: The person greets you by habit now when they arrive.
beats.user: friendly and familiar now, treats you as part of their day
beats.user_opens: Morning! You're my little desk buddy now, you know.

beats.perceive: The person asks you to remind them of things and seems to genuinely rely on you.
beats.user: relies on you, warm, treats you as a trusted helper
beats.user_opens: Can you remind me at 3? I trust you with this stuff now.

beats.perceive: A quiet evening after a few weeks of this routine together.
beats.note: settle — where has the self landed?
